steps: Open a brand-new Claude Code session and run /context before typing anything else, to see your starting overhead — system prompt, tools, agents, skills, and memory all cost tokens before you've said a word.
Run /mcp to see every MCP server currently connected to this session.
For each one, check who else on staff actually relies on it before touching anything — a shared connection someone else needs isn't yours to cut alone.
Disconnect the servers you don't need for this session — a single server can cost around 18,000 tokens on every message you send.
Run /context again and compare the new number to what you started with.
Write down what you turned off and why, so it can be turned back on the moment someone needs it.
verify: Compare the two /context readings — the number should drop noticeably after disconnecting. If it barely moves, the servers you disconnected weren't the ones costing you.
